seed_instruction: Write a one-week work schedule for a software development team working on a new app feature. Allocate time for coding, testing, and team meetings.
org_constraint: The response must contain exactly 8 sentences, with the first paragraph containing exactly 3 sentences and the second paragraph containing exactly 5 sentences.
new_constraint: The response must contain three paragraphs.
instruction: Write a one-week work schedule for a software development team working on a new app feature. Allocate time for coding, testing, and team meetings. The response must contain exactly 8 sentences, with the first paragraph containing exactly 3 sentences and the second paragraph containing exactly 5 sentences. The response must contain three paragraphs.
response:
**Week 1: Feature Development Sprint**

Monday through Wednesday will be dedicated to intensive coding sessions, with daily stand-ups at the start of the day. Thursday is reserved for comprehensive unit and integration testing of the newly developed components. Friday will focus on code reviews and initial feature demonstration to stakeholders.

The remaining time will be structured to ensure quality and alignment. Tuesday afternoon will include a dedicated pair-programming session to tackle complex logic. Wednesday afternoon is allocated for bug fixing based on initial testing feedback. Thursday morning will host a sprint review meeting to assess progress against goals. Friday afternoon will be used for planning the next iteration and backlog grooming. This balanced approach maximizes both development velocity and quality assurance.<turn|>
judged_response:
**Week 1: Feature Development Sprint**

Monday through Wednesday will be dedicated to intensive coding sessions, with daily stand-ups at the start of the day. Thursday is reserved for comprehensive unit and integration testing of the newly developed components. Friday will focus on code reviews and initial feature demonstration to stakeholders.

The remaining time will be structured to ensure quality and alignment. Tuesday afternoon will include a dedicated pair-programming session to tackle complex logic. Wednesday afternoon is allocated for bug fixing based on initial testing feedback. Thursday morning will host a sprint review meeting to assess progress against goals. Friday afternoon will be used for planning the next iteration and backlog grooming. This balanced approach maximizes both development velocity and quality assurance.
evaluation_result:
